vitality.primary_driver_read: sleep, rhythm, metabolic load, and low fitness

optional_actions.label: Physical activity / fitness opportunity pool

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline and cohort evidence strongly support direction, but C1 and C2 QALY magnitudes require causal haircut and utility translation.

overlap_rules: C2 capacity is credited once for the activity/fitness lever, never once per disease domain.
C1 disease-prevention slices are included inside the bundle anchor and should not be added again from legacy physical-activity slices.
C3 resilience is credited once as a general reserve effect, not separately per downstream disease.
Do not rank the bundle itself as a patient instruction when a more atomic action is present.

optional_actions.label: GLP-1/GIP metabolic therapy

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Weight-loss efficacy is RCT-backed; cardiovascular outcome value is high only in close-match established-CVD populations and model-translated in primary prevention.

overlap_rules: Do not double count weight-mediated VO2max improvement already credited to physical activity.
Pair with resistance training or nutrition guardrails when lean-mass loss would reduce capacity value.
Keep adherence and medication access as feasibility axes, not intrinsic QALY haircuts.

optional_actions.label: Supervised resistance training

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

overlap_rules: If the broad physical_activity bundle is active, allocate shared capacity value rather than summing both totals.
When paired with GLP-1/GIP therapy, credit lean-mass preservation once.
Do not substitute generic activity minutes for progressive resistance work.

diagnostics.label: Coronary calcium scoring

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-supported reclassification for uncertain statin decisions; downstream QALY is modeled from lipid/BP intensity changes.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Plaque burden changes lipid/BP intensity, urgency, and sequencing.

diagnostics.label: Home sleep apnea test

evidence_axis.basis_summary: AASM-supported testing in high-pretest OSA phenotypes; value comes from symptom/BP sequencing while CV event prevention is explicitly excluded.

value_channel.basis: D7 mandatory label (trace F6). The 0.45 is defensible as felt-quality (symptom/energy) plus BP-treatment sequencing. It is explicitly NOT cardiovascular-prevention value: CPAP's CV-event evidence is null (SAVE). The same number read as CV prevention would be indefensible; the label is the guardrail on display.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Sleep-disordered breathing confirmed, changing BP sequencing, fatigue/vitality interpretation, and treatment priority.

diagnostics.label: Home or ambulatory BP confirmation

evidence_axis.basis_summary: USPSTF-backed out-of-office confirmation with strong downstream treatment evidence and clear management threshold.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Home or ambulatory BP average confirms persistent hypertension versus white-coat or measurement artifact, changing medication threshold and upstream driver sequencing.

diagnostics.label: ECG / Holter bradycardia workup

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Ambulatory ECG and QT safety monitoring are guideline/consensus-supported when phenotype or medication risk fits, but generic yield and QALY-if are context-heavy.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Clinically meaningful bradyarrhythmia or conduction disease changes exercise clearance, sleep interpretation, cardiology escalation, and safety monitoring.

diagnostics.label: Oral glucose tolerance test

evidence_axis.basis_summary: ADA criteria make OGTT validity strong; yield and downstream value depend on ambiguous-risk phenotype and subsequent treatment path.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Post-load diabetes confirmation changes diabetes labeling, medication eligibility, risk thresholds, and auditability of downstream QALY.

diagnostics.label: FIB-4 then FibroScan if indicated

evidence_axis.basis_summary: AASLD staged fibrosis assessment is guideline-supported in risk-qualified MASLD; QALY-if depends on stage and management actually changed.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Fibrosis risk changes urgency of weight-loss therapy, alcohol guidance, metabolic medication selection, hepatology referral, and monitoring.

required_items.label: Colorectal cancer screening
required_items.reason: Age 47 and colorectal screening not current.

required_items.label: ATM genetic counseling and documentation
required_items.reason: ATM P/LP heterozygous finding requires documented guidance.

required_items.label: ATM family-history gate
required_items.reason: CONSIDER pancreatic surveillance (MRI/MRCP and/or EUS) only if a first- or second-degree relative has had pancreatic cancer

required_findings.interpretation: Use when primary-prevention lipid treatment is indicated by risk, risk enhancer, or shared decision threshold and contraindications are absent.
Use only after ferritin, iron indices, anemia status, and symptom context support iron deficiency as an active driver.
Use only when repeat TSH/free T4 and clinical context support treatment or specialist-directed follow-up.

excluded_items.label: Vitamin D repletion for confirmed deficiency

required_findings.interpretation: Use for confirmed deficiency, especially when bone health, falls risk, or muscle symptoms change the action sequence.

excluded_items.label: BP pharmacotherapy for confirmed stage-2 hypertension

required_findings.interpretation: Use after office, home, or ambulatory readings support persistent stage-2 hypertension and secondary causes or sequencing issues are considered.

excluded_items.label: CBC, iron studies, and thyroid panel

warnings: Non-CVD risk domains remain marked pending live model wiring in source prototype.

vitality.primary_driver_read: iron, thyroid, sleep fragmentation, bone context, and low fitness

optional_actions.label: Physical activity / fitness opportunity pool

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline and cohort evidence strongly support direction, but C1 and C2 QALY magnitudes require causal haircut and utility translation.

overlap_rules: C2 capacity is credited once for the activity/fitness lever, never once per disease domain.
C1 disease-prevention slices are included inside the bundle anchor and should not be added again from legacy physical-activity slices.
C3 resilience is credited once as a general reserve effect, not separately per downstream disease.
Do not rank the bundle itself as a patient instruction when a more atomic action is present.

optional_actions.label: Statin primary prevention

evidence_axis.basis_summary: RCT meta-analysis support for lipid lowering with patient-risk scaling; burden and exact patient QALY remain model-translated but direction is strong when indicated.

overlap_rules: Do not add a C2 healthspan benefit unless a separate patient-specific pathway is established.
Do not truncate fatal-event benefit solely because a cited endpoint is nonfatal.

optional_actions.label: Supervised resistance training

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Resistance training has strong function evidence and supportive observational prevention evidence, but hard-outcome QALY magnitude is translated.

overlap_rules: If the broad physical_activity bundle is active, allocate shared capacity value rather than summing both totals.
When paired with GLP-1/GIP therapy, credit lean-mass preservation once.
Do not substitute generic activity minutes for progressive resistance work.

diagnostics.label: Coronary calcium scoring

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Guideline-supported reclassification for uncertain statin decisions; downstream QALY is modeled from lipid/BP intensity changes.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Plaque burden changes lipid/BP intensity, urgency, and sequencing.

diagnostics.label: CBC, iron studies, and thyroid panel

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Fatigue lab yield is evidence-bounded and low; downstream benefit is real only when true iron deficiency or overt thyroid dysfunction is found.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Iron deficiency, anemia, clinically significant thyroid dysfunction, or a combined reversible fatigue driver changes treatment and training sequence.

diagnostics.label: Vitamin D recheck

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Broad asymptomatic vitamin D screening is not guideline-supported and broad fracture-prevention benefit is not shown.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Confirmed deficiency changes supplement dosing, bone-health sequencing, and resistance-training guardrails.

diagnostics.label: Baseline DEXA

evidence_axis.basis_summary: DXA bone screening is guideline-supported in eligible women/risk groups; lean-mass/body-composition interpretation is weaker and should be split later.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Low bone density, high visceral fat, or low lean mass changes resistance training, nutrition, medication thresholds, and monitoring cadence.

diagnostics.label: Insomnia / sleep regularity screen

evidence_axis.basis_summary: Actigraphy is low-burden and conditionally guideline-supported, but generic sleep-fragmentation reclassification and QALY value are weak compared with phenotype-driven OSA testing.

diagnostics.dominant_reclassification_path: Insomnia, fragmented sleep, menopause symptoms, mood, or schedule irregularity changes recovery intervention and training sequence.

required_findings.interpretation: Use only after ferritin, iron indices, anemia status, and symptom context support iron deficiency as an active driver.
Use only when repeat TSH/free T4 and clinical context support treatment or specialist-directed follow-up.

excluded_items.label: Vitamin D repletion for confirmed deficiency

required_findings.interpretation: Use for confirmed deficiency, especially when bone health, falls risk, or muscle symptoms change the action sequence.
Use when BMI and comorbidity criteria are met and contraindications, access, side effects, lean-mass protection, and durability are addressed.

excluded_items.label: BP pharmacotherapy for confirmed stage-2 hypertension

required_findings.interpretation: Use after office, home, or ambulatory readings support persistent stage-2 hypertension and secondary causes or sequencing issues are considered.
Required preventive care gap. Route as must-do even when ranking and patient-QALY display are separate.
Required genetics guidance and condition-specific surveillance routing. Specific actions depend on sex, family history, variant context, and current guidelines.
